description: Generate feature/flow documentation for a repo from scratch, FlowDoc-style (document what the code DOES, not a file inventory). Spawns: root epic -> a single discover spawner -> one doc bead per manifest item (created at runtime) -> a finalize bead gated on all the discovered docs. Replaces the flowdoc / flowdoc-user agent-loop. Pour with --var audience=maintainer (default) or --var audience=user. Run via 'bd mol pour flowdoc-generate --var audience=...'.

audience.description: maintainer = developer docs in __docs/ (implementation detail, file refs); user = end-user docs in _docs/ (no code/paths, task-oriented voice).

steps.title: FlowDoc: generate {{audience}} documentation
steps.description:
Umbrella for a from-scratch FlowDoc documentation pass ({{audience}} edition).

AUDIENCE MAP (read this first, every child obeys it):
  - audience = maintainer -> write to __docs/ ; voice = for developers;
    include implementation detail, file paths, mermaid, API/View tables.
  - audience = user       -> write to _docs/  ; voice = for end users;
    NO source paths, NO code, NO internal URLs; task/outcome oriented.

This epic has exactly two authored children: 'discover' (runs first and spawns
one bead per documentation item) and 'finalize' (runs last, gated on every
spawned doc). Do not work this epic directly — work its leaves.

steps.title: FlowDoc {{audience}}: discover & scaffold, then spawn one bead per doc
steps.description:
DISCOVERY + FAN-OUT (one bead). Your bead id is the value shown as
"Complete beads issue <id>" at the top of this goal — call it $SELF below.

AUDIENCE = {{audience}}.  DOCS_DIR = __docs/ if maintainer, _docs/ if user.

== Part A: survey & scaffold (do all of this, then spawn, then stop) ==
1. list_files(".", recursive=True); read dependency manifests, configs, entry
   points. Identify tech stack, features, flows, API endpoints, views/pages,
   cross-cutting concepts.
2. Write DOCS_DIR/Architecture.md (maintainer) or DOCS_DIR/Overview.md (user):
   exec summary, tech stack table, system mermaid diagram, feature table, key
   flows, external deps. (user edition: plain-language, no file paths.)
3. Write DOCS_DIR/_FlowDocGuide.md — the authoring contract every doc bead
   follows. It MUST contain, verbatim, these section templates so a generic
   agent produces output identical in shape to the FlowDoc agents:
     * Feature: What it does / Why it exists / How it works (User + System
       perspective) / mermaid sequence / Implementation Map table / Config /
       Edge Cases (> [!WARNING]) / Error Scenarios / Testing / Related.
     * Flow: What happens / Trigger / Outcome / mermaid flowchart / Step-by-step
       (What/Where/Failure mode) / Data Transformations / Failure Handling /
       Debugging / Related.
     * Endpoint: METHOD+path overview table / Request (headers,params,body,
       validation) / Response (success+errors) / Implementation Map / mermaid /
       curl example / Related.   (maintainer only)
     * View: route overview / what it does / user actions / page-structure
       mermaid / components / state / API deps / Related.   (maintainer only)
     * Concept: What is it / Why this approach / How it works + example /
       Where used / Conventions (> [!IMPORTANT]) / Anti-patterns (> [!CAUTION]).
     * User Guide (user edition): goal / prerequisites / numbered steps with
       expected result / troubleshooting / related — NO code, NO file paths.
   Rules to encode in the guide: features-not-files; flows-not-functions;
   why-over-what; link every reference; mermaid for flows; callouts for
   gotchas; READ-ONLY source; output only under DOCS_DIR.
4. Write DOCS_DIR/_Manifest.md: progress counters + every item to document,
   grouped (Features / Flows / Endpoints / Views / Concepts), each as
   "- [ ] NNN | <Type>: <Name> -> [Name](<Section>/<Name>)".
5. Write the index.md files: DOCS_DIR/index.md plus one per section dir.

== Part B: fan out — ONE bead per manifest item (the deadlock-safe contract) ==
For EACH unchecked item in _Manifest.md, create exactly one child bead:
  bd create --type=task --priority=2 --parent=$SELF \
    --title='FlowDoc {{audience}}: <Type>: <Name>' \
    --description='Write DOCS_DIR/<Section>/<Name>.md following the <Type>
       template in DOCS_DIR/_FlowDocGuide.md for audience={{audience}}.
       Read the relevant source fresh, fill every template section, link
       related docs, then tick this item -> [x] in _Manifest.md and bump the
       counters. Acceptance: file exists, all template sections filled (no
       placeholders), links valid, manifest updated.'
WIRING RULES — violating these creates deadlocks, so obey exactly:
  - Parent EVERY spawned bead to $SELF (so the finalize fan-out gate counts it).
  - Do NOT add any dependency from a doc bead to the finalize bead.
  - Do NOT add blocks edges between doc beads — they are independent/parallel.
  - Do NOT create your own finalize/rollup bead; one already exists and waits.
  - Spawn AT LEAST ONE child (a tiny repo still gets >=1 doc).
  - Do NOT close any bead — the LLM judges close. (bead-chain blocks bd close.)

Stop after scaffolding + spawning. Summarize what you wrote and how many doc
beads you spawned so the judges can verify.

steps.title: FlowDoc {{audience}}: finalize docs (troubleshooting, indexes, links)
steps.description:
FINALIZE the {{audience}} documentation set. You only become ready once
'discover' and EVERY doc bead it spawned are closed (fan-out gate).

DOCS_DIR = __docs/ if maintainer, _docs/ if user.
1. Confirm every item in DOCS_DIR/_Manifest.md is checked [x]; fix counters.
2. Write/refresh DOCS_DIR/Troubleshooting.md (maintainer) or DOCS_DIR/FAQ.md
   (user) from gotchas surfaced while documenting.
3. Verify all intra-doc links resolve and every section index lists its docs.
4. (user edition only) Grep the docs for developer leakage — source paths,
   shell/install commands, localhost/staging URLs, class/function names — and
   replace with user-facing equivalents.
READ-ONLY source. Output only under DOCS_DIR. Do NOT close any bead yourself.
